1866.             Default is <see langword="true"/>.
1867.             </value>
1868.         </member>
1869.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict">
1870.             <summary>
1871.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether an exception is thrown if the underlying AI service indicates that it manages chat history
1872.             (for example, by returning a conversation id in the response), but a <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> is configured for the agent.
1873.             </summary>
1874.             <value>
1875.             Default is <see langword="true"/>.
1876.             </value>
1877.         </member>
1878.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence">
1879.             <summary>
1880.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> should persist
1881.             chat history after each individual service call within the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/>
1882.             loop, rather than at the end of the full agent run.
1883.             </summary>
1884.             <remarks>
1885.             <para>
1886.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/>
1887.             decorator becomes active in the chat client pipeline. It handles two complementary scenarios:
1888.             </para>
1889.             <list type="bullet">
1890.             <item>
1891.             <term>Framework-managed chat history</term>
1892.             <description>
1893.             The decorator loads history from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> before each service call
1894.             and persists new request and response messages after each call. It returns a sentinel
1895.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ConversationId"/> on the response, causing the
1896.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> to treat the conversation as service-managed — clearing
1897.             accumulated history between iterations and not injecting duplicate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
1898.             during approval-response processing.
1899.             </description>
1900.             </item>
1901.             <item>
1902.             <term>AI Service-stored chat history</term>
1903.             <description>
1904.             When the service manages its own chat history (returning a real <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ConversationId"/>),
1905.             the decorator updates <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> after each service call so
1906.             that intermediate ConversationId changes are captured immediately. For some services (e.g., the
1907.             Conversations API with the Responses API), there is only one thread with one ID, so every service
1908.             call updates it anyway and updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> has little effect
1909.             since it's the same ID. For other services (e.g., Responses API with Response IDs), a new ID is generated
1910.             with each service call, so updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> ensures that the
1911.             latest ID is always captured, even mid-run.
1912.             Enabling this option ensures consistent per-service-call behavior across all service types.
1913.             </description>
1914.             </item>
1915.             </list>
1916.             <para>
1917.             When set to <see langword="false"/> (the default), the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> handles
1918.             chat history persistence at the end of the full agent run via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> if using
1919.             framework-managed chat history. For AI service-stored chat history, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/>
1920.             updates happen only at the end of the run.
1921.             </para>
1922.             <para>
1923.             When setting the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs"/> setting to <see langword="true"/> and
1924.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence"/> to <see langword="true"/>, ensure that your custom chat client stack includes a
1925.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/> to enable per-service-call persistence.
1926.             If no <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/> is provided, and you are not storing chat history via other means,
1927.             no chat history may be stored.
1928.             When using a custom chat client stack, you can add a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/>
1929.             manually via the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilderExtensions.UsePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilder)"/>
1930.             extension method.
1931.             </para>
1932.             </remarks>
1933.             <value>
1934.             Default is <see langword="false"/>.
1935.             </value>
1936.         </member>
1937.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.EnableMessageInjection">
1938.             <summary>
1939.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/>
1940.             in the chat client pipeline.
1941.             </summary>
1942.             <remarks>
1943.             <para>
1944.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/> is added to the pipeline
1945.             between the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> and the inner client. This enables external code
1946.             (such as tool delegates) to inject messages into the function execution loop via the
1947.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/> class, which can be resolved from the chat client using
1948.             <c>GetService<MessageInjectingChatClient>()</c>.
1949.             </para>
1950.             <para>
1951.             This setting can be used independently of <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence"/>,
1952.             however it is recommended to also enable per-service-call persistence when using message injection
1953.             so that injected messages are persisted to chat history between service calls.
1954.             </para>
1955.             <para>
1956.             When setting the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs"/> setting to <see langword="true"/> and
1957.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.EnableMessageInjection"/> to <see langword="true"/>, ensure that your custom chat client stack
1958.             includes a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/>. You can add one manually via the
1959.             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilderExtensions.UseMessageInjection(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilder)"/> extension method.
1960.             </para>
1961.             </remarks>
1962.             <value>
1963.             Default is <see langword="false"/>.
1964.             </value>
1965.         </member>
1966.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Clone">
1967.             <summary>
1968.             Creates a new instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions"/> with the same values as this instance.
1969.             </summary>
1970.         </member>

             <term>Framework-managed chat history</term>
             <description>
             The decorator loads history from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> before each service call
             and persists new request and response messages after each call. It returns a sentinel
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ConversationId"/> on the response, causing the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> to treat the conversation as service-managed — clearing
             accumulated history between iterations and not injecting duplicate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
             during approval-response processing.
             </description>
             </item>
             <item>
             <term>AI Service-stored chat history</term>
             <description>
             When the service manages its own chat history (returning a real <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ConversationId"/>),
             the decorator updates <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> after each service call so
             that intermediate ConversationId changes are captured immediately. For some services (e.g., the
             Conversations API with the Responses API), there is only one thread with one ID, so every service
             call updates it anyway and updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> has little effect
             since it's the same ID. For other services (e.g., Responses API with Response IDs), a new ID is generated
             with each service call, so updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/> ensures that the
             latest ID is always captured, even mid-run.
             Enabling this option ensures consistent per-service-call behavior across all service types.
             </description>
             </item>
             </list>
             <para>
             When set to <see langword="false"/> (the default), the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> handles
             chat history persistence at the end of the full agent run via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> if using
             framework-managed chat history. For AI service-stored chat history, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId"/>
             updates happen only at the end of the run.
             </para>
             <para>
             When setting the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs"/> setting to <see langword="true"/> and
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence"/> to <see langword="true"/>, ensure that your custom chat client stack includes a
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/> to enable per-service-call persistence.
             If no <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/> is provided, and you are not storing chat history via other means,
             no chat history may be stored.
             When using a custom chat client stack, you can add a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/>
             manually via the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilderExtensions.UsePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilder)"/>
             extension method.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <value>
             Default is <see langword="false"/>.
             </value>
         </member>
         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.EnableMessageInjection">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/>
             in the chat client pipeline.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When set to <see langword="true"/>, a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/> is added to the pipeline
             between the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> and the inner client. This enables external code
             (such as tool delegates) to inject messages into the function execution loop via the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/> class, which can be resolved from the chat client using
             <c>GetService<MessageInjectingChatClient>()</c>.
             </para>
             <para>
             This setting can be used independently of <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence"/>,
             however it is recommended to also enable per-service-call persistence when using message injection
             so that injected messages are persisted to chat history between service calls.
             </para>
             <para>
             When setting the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs"/> setting to <see langword="true"/> and
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.EnableMessageInjection"/> to <see langword="true"/>, ensure that your custom chat client stack
             includes a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.MessageInjectingChatClient"/>. You can add one manually via the
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilderExtensions.UseMessageInjection(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatClientBuilder)"/> extension method.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <value>
             Default is <see langword="false"/>.
             </value>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Clone">
             <summary>
             Creates a new instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions"/> with the same values as this instance.
             </summary>
         </member>

result.content:
4250.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
4251.             <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
4252.             <remarks>
4253.             <para>
4254.             The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
4255.             (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
4256.             automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
4257.             and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
4258.             These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
4259.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
4260.             interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
4261.             via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
4262.             respectively.
4263.             </para>
4264.             </remarks>
4265.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
4266.         </member>
4267.         <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory._defaultOptions">
4268.             <summary>Holds the default options instance used when creating function.</summary>
4269.         </member>
4270.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Delegate,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions)">
4271.             <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via a delegate.</summary>
4272.             <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
4273.             <param name="options">Metadata to use to override defaults inferred from <paramref name="method"/>.</param>
4274.             <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
4275.             <remarks>
4276.             <para>
4277.             By default, any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
4278.             of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
4279.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:

             of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
             <list type="bullet">

                 not included in the generated JSON schema.
               </item>
               <item>
                 By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                 and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>

                 passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and are not included in the JSON schema. If the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>
                 instance passed to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is <see langword="null"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> implementation
                 manufactures an empty instance, such that parameters of type <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> can always be satisfied, whether
                 optional or not.

             <para>

             </para>

             unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of <paramref name="method"/>, consider having <paramref name="method"/>
             point to an instance method on an instance configured to hold the appropriate state. An <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameter can also be

             </para>

1565.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions">
1566.             <summary>
1567.             Provides optional parameters and configuration settings for controlling agent run behavior.
1568.             </summary>
1569.             <remarks>
1570.             <para>
1571.             Implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> may provide subclasses of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> with additional options specific to that agent type.
1572.             </para>
1573.             </remarks>
1574.         </member>
1575.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.#ctor">
1576.             <summary>
1577.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> class.
1578.             </summary>
1579.         </member>
1580.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.#ctor(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions)">
1581.             <summary>
1582.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> class by copying values from the specified options.
1583.             </summary>
1584.             <param name="options">The options instance from which to copy values.</param>
1585.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="options"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1586.         </member>
1587.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ContinuationToken">
1588.             <summary>
1589.             Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming and getting the result of the agent response identified by this token.
1590.             </summary>
1591.             <remarks>
1592.             This property is used for background responses that can be activated via the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses"/>
1593.             property if the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> implementation supports them.
1594.             Streamed background responses, such as those returned by default by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
1595.             can be resumed if interrupted. This means that a continuation token obtained from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseUpdate.ContinuationToken"/>
1596.             of an update just before the interruption occurred can be passed to this property to resume the stream from the point of interruption.
1597.             Non-streamed background responses, such as those returned by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
1598.             can be polled for completion by obtaining the token from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse.ContinuationToken"/> property
1599.             and passing it via this property on subsequent calls to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
1600.             </remarks>
1601.         </member>
1602.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
1603.             <summary>
1604.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.
1605.             </summary>
1606.             <remarks>
1607.             <para>
1608.             Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
1609.             and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
1610.             </para>
1611.             <para>
1612.             When this property is set to true, non-streaming APIs may start a background operation and return an initial
1613.             response with a continuation token. Subsequent calls to the same API should be made in a polling manner with
1614.             the continuation token to get the final result of the operation.
1615.             </para>
1616.             <para>
1617.             When this property is set to true, streaming APIs may also start a background operation and begin streaming
1618.             response updates until the operation is completed. If the streaming connection is interrupted, the
1619.             continuation token obtained from the last update that has one should be supplied to a subsequent call to the same streaming API
1620.             to resume the stream from the point of interruption and continue receiving updates until the operation is completed.
1621.             </para>
1622.             <para>
1623.             This property only takes effect if the implementation it's used with supports background responses.
1624.             If the implementation does not support background responses, this property will be ignored.
1625.             </para>
1626.             </remarks>
1627.         </member>
1628.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties">
1629.             <summary>
1630.             Gets or sets additional properties associated with these options.
1631.             </summary>
1632.             <value>
1633.             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AdditionalPropertiesDictionary"/> containing custom properties,
1634.             or <see langword="null"/> if no additional properties are present.
1635.             </value>
1636.             <remarks>
1637.             Additional properties provide a way to include custom metadata or provider-specific
1638.             information that doesn't fit into the standard options schema. This is useful for
1639.             preserving implementation-specific details or extending the options with custom data.
1640.             </remarks>
1641.         </member>
1642.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.ResponseFormat">
1643.             <summary>
1644.             Gets or sets the response format.
1645.             </summary>
1646.             <remarks>
1647.             If <see langword="null"/>, no response format is specified and the agent will use its default.
1648.             This property can be set to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Text"/> to specify that the response should be unstructured text,
1649.             to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Json"/> to specify that the response should be structured JSON data, or
1650.             an instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormatJson"/> constructed with a specific JSON schema to request that the
1651.             response be structured JSON data according to that schema. It is up to the agent implementation if or how
1652.             to honor the request. If the agent implementation doesn't recognize the specific kind of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat"/>,
1653.             it can be ignored.
1654.             </remarks>
1655.         </member>
1656.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone">
1657.             <summary>
1658.             Produces a clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
1659.             </summary>
1660.             <returns>
1661.             A clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
1662.             </returns>
1663.             <remarks>
1664.             <para>
1665.             The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>,
1666.             are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created, but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
1667.             </para>
1668.             <para>
1669.             Derived types should override <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone"/> to return an instance of the derived type.
1670.             </para>
1671.             </remarks>
1672.         </member>
1673.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession">
1674.             <summary>
1675.             Base abstraction for all agent threads.
1676.             </summary>
1677.             <remarks>
1678.             <para>
1679.             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> contains the state of a specific conversation with an agent which may include:
1680.             <list type="bullet">
1681.             <item><description>Conversation history or a reference to externally stored conversation history.</description></item>
1682.             <item><description>Memories or a reference to externally stored memories.</description></item>
1683.             <item><description>Any other state that the agent needs to persist across runs for a conversation.</description></item>
1684.             </list>
1685.             </para>
1686.             <para>
1687.             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may also have behaviors attached to it that may include:
1688.             <list type="bullet">
1689.             <item><description>Customized storage of state.</description></item>
1690.             <item><description>Data extraction from and injection into a conversation.</description></item>
1691.             <item><description>Chat history reduction, e.g. where messages needs to be summarized or truncated to reduce the size.</description></item>
1692.             </list>
1693.             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> is always constructed by an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> so that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>
1694.             can attach any necessary behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/>. See the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.CreateSessionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
1695.             and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.DeserializeSessionAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> methods for more information.
1696.             </para>
1697.             <para>
1698.             Because of these behaviors, an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may not be reusable across different agents, since each agent
1699.             may add different behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> it creates.
1700.             </para>

             If <see langword="null"/>, no response format is specified and the agent will use its default.
             This property can be set to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Text"/> to specify that the response should be unstructured text,
             to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat.Json"/> to specify that the response should be structured JSON data, or
             an instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormatJson"/> constructed with a specific JSON schema to request that the
             response be structured JSON data according to that schema. It is up to the agent implementation if or how
             to honor the request. If the agent implementation doesn't recognize the specific kind of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatResponseFormat"/>,
             it can be ignored.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone">
             <summary>
             Produces a clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
             </summary>
             <returns>
             A clone of the current <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions"/> instance.
             </returns>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>,
             are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created, but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
             </para>
             <para>
             Derived types should override <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone"/> to return an instance of the derived type.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession">
             <summary>
             Base abstraction for all agent threads.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> contains the state of a specific conversation with an agent which may include:
             <list type="bullet">
             <item><description>Conversation history or a reference to externally stored conversation history.</description></item>
             <item><description>Memories or a reference to externally stored memories.</description></item>
             <item><description>Any other state that the agent needs to persist across runs for a conversation.</description></item>
             </list>
             </para>
             <para>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may also have behaviors attached to it that may include:
             <list type="bullet">
             <item><description>Customized storage of state.</description></item>
             <item><description>Data extraction from and injection into a conversation.</description></item>
             <item><description>Chat history reduction, e.g. where messages needs to be summarized or truncated to reduce the size.</description></item>
             </list>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> is always constructed by an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> so that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>
             can attach any necessary behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/>. See the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.CreateSessionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.DeserializeSessionAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> methods for more information.
             </para>
             <para>
             Because of these behaviors, an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may not be reusable across different agents, since each agent
             may add different behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> it creates.
             </para>

I've successfully explored the **Microsoft.Agents.AI NuGet package** API surface. Here are the exact class names and method signatures you need:

### **1. Main Agent Class: `ChatClientAgent`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Agents.AI`

  ```csharp

  ChatClientAgent(IChatClient, ChatClientAgentOptions, ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory, IServiceProvider? services)
  ```

### **2. Tool/Function Definition: `AIFunctionFactory`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Static Methods**:
  ```csharp
  AIFunctionFactory.Create(Delegate method, AIFunctionFactoryOptions? options)
  AIFunctionFactory.Create(Delegate method, string? name, string? description, JsonSerializerOptions? serializerOptions)

  AIFunctionFactory.Create(MethodInfo method, object? target, string? name, string? description, JsonSerializerOptions? serializerOptions)
  ```
  - Returns: `AIFunction` (which extends `AIFunctionDeclaration` and is invokable)

### **3. Invoke Agent: `ChatClientAgent.RunAsync()`**
- **Overloads**:
  ```csharp
  Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(AgentSession session, ChatClientAgentRunOptions? options, CancellationToken ct)
  Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(string message, AgentSession session, ChatClientAgentRunOptions? options, CancellationToken ct)
  Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(ChatMessage message, AgentSession session, ChatClientAgentRunOptions? options, CancellationToken ct)
  Task<AgentResponse> RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, AgentSession session, ChatClientAgentRunOptions? options, CancellationToken ct)
  IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate> RunStreamingAsync(...) // Streaming versions
  ```

### **4. Invocation Options: `ChatClientAgentRunOptions`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Agents.AI`
- **Properties**:
  ```csharp
  ChatOptions? ChatOptions { get; set; }
  Func<IChatClient, IChatClient>? ChatClientFactory { get; set; }
  ```
- **No direct iteration limit property** - control via:
  - `ChatOptions.MaxOutputTokens` (int?)
  - `ChatOptions.AllowMultipleToolCalls` (bool?)
  - Internal agent loop termination logic

### **5. Agent Configuration: `ChatClientAgentOptions`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Agents.AI`
- **Key Properties**:
  ```csharp
  string? Id, Name, Description
  ChatOptions? ChatOptions
  ChatHistoryProvider? ChatHistoryProvider
  List<AIContextProvider>? AIContextProviders
  bool UseProvidedChatClientAsIs { get; set; }
  bool RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence { get; set; }
  bool EnableMessageInjection { get; set; }
  ```

### **6. Session Management: `ChatClientAgentSession`**
- **Constructor**: `ChatClientAgentSession()`
- **Properties**: `string? ConversationId { get; set; }`

### **7. Chat Options: `ChatOptions`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Key Properties**:
  ```csharp
  string? ConversationId, Instructions, ModelId
  double? Temperature, TopP
  int? MaxOutputTokens, TopK, Seed
  IList<AITool>? Tools
  ChatToolMode? ToolMode
  bool? AllowMultipleToolCalls
  ```

### **8. Message: `ChatMessage`**
- **Namespace**: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Constructor**: `ChatMessage(ChatRole role, string text)` or `ChatMessage(ChatRole role, object? content)`

### **Important Notes:**
- ✅ **No iteration limit in `AgentRunOptions`** - you control this via `MaxOutputTokens` or implement your own wrapper
- ✅ **AIFunction** is created via `AIFunctionFactory.Create()` and is an `AITool` that's invokable
- ✅ Tools are registered via `ChatOptions.Tools` collection (IList<AITool>)
- ✅ Agent automatically invokes function calls through the `FunctionInvokingChatClient` decorator

result = subprocess.run(['strings', 'Microsoft.Agents.AI.dll'], capture_output=True, text=True)
lines = result.stdout.split('\n')

for l in lines:
    if 'Session' in l or 'AgentResponse' in l or 'RunAsync' in l or 'RunStreaming' in l:
        print(l)
" 2>/dev/null | head -30

data.reasoningText:
I need to fix several issues:
1. Missing `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Console` package for `AddLogging`
2. Missing `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` package for `BuildServiceProvider`
3. `UseOpenAIChatClient` - need to check correct API for the chat client builder
4. `ChatClientAgentSession` constructor
5. `AgentResponseUpdate` doesn't have `Content` property

Let me explore the actual APIs more carefully.

done

# Also check the abstractions
